no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly tells the agent exactly what to do: 'Rename listTopLevelFiles() to listTopLevelEntries()', 'Update the logic to extract the first path component', 'Use a Set instead of array for O(1) lookups', 'remove bench_util, add tests/bench_util', 'Update deno_bench_util path dependency to point to ./tests/bench_util'. These are precise implementation instructions, not just descriptions of what's broken.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The tests for lint.js (test_lint_js_detects_top_level_directories, test_lint_js_blocks_unauthorized_top_level) write their own standalone test scripts rather than importing from tools/lint.js, so they don't assert on the agent's specific implementation in lint.js. The Cargo.toml and file-move tests check behavioral outcomes (workspace members, file existence, cargo check passing) rather than exact implementation details. An alternative correct fix would pass these tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute cargo check subprocess (test_cargo_check_passes, test_bench_util_compiles), run Node.js scripts that actually call git and process files (test_lint_js_detects_top_level_directories, test_lint_js_blocks_unauthorized_top_level), parse TOML structure (test_cargo_toml_valid), and check file existence (test_bench_util_moved). Only test_docs_tsgo_moved is purely text-based assertion, but most tests verify behavior through execution.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail_to_pass tests require real changes: test_cargo_check_passes requires bench_util to exist at tests/bench_util with correct Cargo.toml references; test_bench_util_compiles requires the crate to actually compile at the new path; test_lint_js_blocks_unauthorized_top_level would fail if bench_util still exists at the old location (it would be an unauthorized top-level entry). A stub/no-op implementation cannot satisfy these.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors from the instruction are tested: bench_util move (test_bench_util_moved, test_bench_util_compiles, test_cargo_check_passes), Cargo.toml updates (test_cargo_toml_valid), docs/tsgo.md move (test_docs_tsgo_moved), and lint.js functionality (test_lint_js_detects_top_level_directories, test_lint_js_blocks_unauthorized_top_level). The lint.js function renaming and Set usage aren't directly tested but those are implementation details.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments ('Gates (pass_to_pass, static)', 'Fail-to-pass (pr_diff)'), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining each test's purpose.

structured_data_schema.reason: The task involves file moves and code refactoring, not producing structured data output.
